name: MCP Tool Marketplace Deployment
description: Three deployment models for MCP servers: embedded MCP client (low latency, high overhead), pooled server instances (efficient, careful isolation required), tenant-dedicated servers (maximum isolation, high cost). Pooled model recommended for standard/free tiers, dedicated for enterprise.

name: sse-bridge-strands-to-vercel-dsp
description: Bridge package translates Strands StreamEvents to Vercel AI SDK Data Stream Protocol over SSE. Critical: tool_use stop reason must NOT emit finish (agent loop continues). Track messageStarted flag to prevent duplicate starts in multi-step loops. Support toolResult, toolStream, stepStart events, and reasoning blocks.

name: express-sse-streaming-gateway
description: Express gateway bridges Vercel AI SDK clients to Strands agents via SSE. Pattern: (1) Extract tenant context from headers, (2) Create ChimeraAgent with tenant isolation, (3) Map StreamEvents to StrandsStreamEvent, (4) Stream via streamStrandsToDSP. Routes: POST /chat/stream (SSE), POST /chat/message (JSON), GET /health.

name: sse-testing-supertest-limitation
description: Supertest (Express testing library) doesn't handle SSE streams well - tests timeout waiting for stream completion. SSE streams don't naturally 'complete' until explicitly closed. Solution: Skip SSE integration tests in unit test suite, rely on manual testing or dedicated SSE test tools. Non-streaming routes test normally.

name: typescript-monorepo-path-resolution
description: TypeScript composite builds with workspace packages require both 'references' and 'paths' in root tsconfig.json. Project references establish build order, but path mappings (baseUrl + paths) are needed for module resolution of @package-name imports.

name: aws-sdk-multi-tenant-integration
description: Multi-tenant AWS SDK integration patterns: (1) IAM role-based auth (no credentials), (2) STS AssumeRole with session tags for tenant isolation, (3) Regional client caching (singleton factory), (4) Exponential backoff with jitter for retries, (5) Cost attribution via request/resource tags. Covers boto3 (Python), AWS SDK v3 (TypeScript), AWS CLI wrapper.

name: aws-mcp-integration
description: AWS services exposed as MCP tools via boto3 (Lambda) or AWS SDK v3 (AgentCore Runtime). Per-tenant IAM roles enforce least-privilege access. AgentCore Gateway provides centralized tool registry with Cedar policy enforcement. Step Functions for structured workflows, EventBridge for reactive patterns, SQS/SNS for async communication, DynamoDB for state persistence.

name: slack-webhook-security
description: Slack webhooks require (1) URL verification challenge response, (2) HMAC-SHA256 signature verification using timingSafeEqual for constant-time comparison, (3) timestamp validation to prevent replay attacks (reject >5min old), (4) bot message filtering to prevent infinite loops.

name: sse-web-chat-reconnection
description: Web chat over SSE implements Vercel AI SDK Data Stream Protocol (DSP) with exponential backoff reconnection (max 5 attempts, 2s base delay), connection health checks via /health endpoint, and graceful handling of network online/offline events.

name: slack-webhook-security-pattern
description: Slack Events API: (1) URL verification first, (2) HMAC-SHA256 signature with timingSafeEqual, (3) 5-min replay protection, (4) Bot filtering, (5) Always respond 200.

content: tenant-provisioning-api: Administrative REST API for tenant lifecycle: POST /tenants (create), GET /tenants/:id (profile), PATCH /tenants/:id (update), POST /tenants/:id/suspend, POST /tenants/:id/activate, GET /tenants/query/tier/:tier, GET /tenants/query/status/:status. No tenant context required (administrative endpoints).

name: gateway-tenant-middleware-pattern
description: Express middleware extracts tenant context from headers (X-Tenant-Id, X-User-Id, X-Tenant-Tier) and validates against DynamoDB using TenantService. Status checks reject SUSPENDED tenants with 403. Mock DynamoDB clients enable development without AWS infrastructure.

name: admin-ui-oauth-flow-pattern
description: Admin UI for chat platform integrations follows OAuth redirect flow pattern: (1) UI initiates OAuth with POST /integrations/:tenantId/:platform (returns authUrl), (2) Platform redirects back with code and state params, (3) UI calls callback endpoint POST /integrations/:tenantId/:platform/callback to exchange code for token, (4) Integration stored in DynamoDB with encrypted access token. State parameter provides CSRF protection.

content: user-pairing-cross-platform-identity: UserPairingService maps platform user IDs (Slack U12345) to Cognito users via DynamoDB items with PK=USER_PAIRING#{platform}#{userId}, SK=COGNITO#{sub}. GSI1 on TENANT#{tenantId} for reverse lookup. resolveUser middleware injects tenant context from paired identity. Fail-closed in production, fallback context in dev.

gateway-mcp-tool-priming: AgentCore Gateway module at packages/core/src/gateway/ maps 25 AWS services (19 aws-tools + 6 discovery) to MCP targets with tier-based access. ToolTier 1 (basic+): s3, ec2, lambda, cloudwatch, sqs. Tier 2 (advanced+): rds, redshift, athena, glue, opensearch. Tier 3 (enterprise+): bedrock, sagemaker, stepfunctions, rekognition, textract, transcribe, codebuild, codecommit, codepipeline. Discovery (all tiers): config-scanner, cost-analyzer, resource-explorer, resource-index, stack-inventory, tag-organizer. Uses TenantTier from @chimera/shared. Counts: basic=11, advanced=16, enterprise/dedicated=25.

name: chat-platform-adapter-pattern
description: PlatformAdapter interface enables platform-agnostic chat integration. parseIncoming() normalizes platform payloads to ChatMessage[], formatResponse() handles platform-specific formatting (Slack Block Kit, Discord embeds, Teams Adaptive Cards, Telegram Markdown). UserPairingService maps platform users to Cognito via USER_PAIRING#{platform}#{platformUserId} key pattern.

name: chat-platform-implementation-order
description: Recommended platform implementation order based on setup complexity: (1) Telegram (2-3d, simplest - REST API only, BotFather setup), (2) Discord (3-5d, moderate - WebSocket gateway, OAuth2, privileged intents), (3) Microsoft Teams (5-7d, complex - Azure Bot Service, Azure AD, Adaptive Cards). All use same PlatformAdapter interface, complexity is in platform ecosystem not adapter code.
